pages.text: Smithery replaces fixed recipes with $(l)parts$(). Every tool and armor piece is assembled from interchangeable parts, and every part carries the stats and traits of its material.$(br2)An $(l)iron$() pick head mines like iron. Put it on $(l)slime$() handles and the tool bounces back from its own mistakes. Materials matter more than shapes.
pages.title: Tools From Parts

pages.text: 1. Build a $(l)Forge$() and melt raw materials into molten metal$(br)2. $(l)Cast$() parts on the Casting Table — or $(l)press$() non-metals on the Part Press$(br)3. $(l)Assemble$() the parts in any crafting grid$(br)4. $(l)Upgrade$() at an anvil: modifiers, repairs, embossments$(br2)Each step has its own chapter in this guide.
pages.title: The Loop
